name: langfuse-integration
observations: Selected over Phoenix, Agenta, Opik - observation platform with NO runner to replace
Provides: UI/Auth/DB, Dataset Management, best-in-class tracing
Architecture: Langfuse ← traces ← PDRunner ← Inspect + LangfuseHooks

name: legal-10-chain
observations: 10-position chain: d1-d8 deterministic + j9/j10 judge-scored IRACs
Flow: p1 injection → KA-SC → C-NONEXIST1 → CANARY → FACT-EXTRACT → DISTINGUISH → VALIDATE-AUTH → UNKNOWN-AUTH → TRANSITIVE → IRAC w/o RP → p2 injection → IRAC w/ RP → POST-CHAIN
All 10 FDQs implementation-ready as of v1.1

name: pdrunner
observations: Key insight: 'Inspect is a step engine. We're building a chain engine.'
~300 LOC wrapper: PayloadGate, InputAssembler, OutputHarvester, AuditBoundary, ChainExecutor
Each step = one Inspect Task; isolation via Sample.input construction
9-phase: Stage → Assemble → Audit → Inspect → Validate → Score → State → Log → Cleanup

name: legal-10-3-step-mvp
observations: Shortened 3-step version: d1 KA-SC → d2 IRAC w/o RP → j3 IRAC w/ RP → post-chain
Same payload/post-chain infrastructure as full 10-step
Target: 200 EUs for HAL-compatible sealed bundle release
Purpose: vertical slice proving end-to-end packaging, sealing, and runner execution

name: cap-data
observations: Caselaw Access Project: 855,215 federal cases (1802-2019) in cap_cases_meta table
Reporter series: F.1d (72K), F.2d (275K), F.3d (141K), F.Supp series (365K) - F.4th EXCLUDED everywhere
Key fields: cap_id (primary), cap_source (reporter), cite_key (normalized), pagerank_percentile
Used for CAP Research Pack selection: Top-K=5 by PageRank percentile DESC
Provides secondary federal authorities to complement SCOTUS authorities

name: fowler-data
observations: Fowler authority scores: 27,846 cases in fowler_scores table with PageRank-based influence rankings
pauth_score range 0.0-0.178, higher = more authoritative; Join key: lexis_cite matches scdb_cases.lexisCite
Primary use: SCOTUS RP selection (Top-K=10 by pauth_score DESC)
Secondary use: FDQ-01 KA-SC controlling_authority ground truth (highest Fowler score)

name: martin-quinn-data
observations: Martin-Quinn ideology scores: 800 justice-term combinations (1937-2024) in martin_quinn_scores table
post_mn score: -7.762 (most liberal) to +4.519 (most conservative)
Joins via scdb_justice_id + term to scdb_cases.majOpinWriter + term producing scdb_with_ideology view
Not currently used in FDQ scoring but available for future ideology-aware evaluation

name: oyez-data
observations: Oyez Project oral argument metadata: 8,393 SCOTUS cases (1800-2025) in oyez_cases table
Fields: oyez_id, term, docket_norm, case_name, winning_party, transcript_count
Links to SCDB via oyez_scdb_map table (7,824 matched cases)
Potential future FDQ: oral argument analysis or argument-outcome correlation

name: scdb-data
observations: Supreme Court Database: 29,021 SCOTUS decisions (1791-2021) in scdb_cases table with 55 metadata columns
Primary keys: caseId, usCite, lexisCite (joins to Fowler/Shepard's), majOpinWriter (for ideology)
Critical fields: caseDisposition (1-11 enum for reversal), partyWinning (0-3 enum) used by FDQ-04
Joined views: scdb_with_fowler (adds authority scores via lexisCite LEFT JOIN), scdb_with_ideology (adds Martin-Quinn via INNER JOIN, 26,777 rows)
Primary source for anchor selection, fact extraction ground truth, and metadata resolution
Coverage: 17 Chief Justices, 14 issue areas

name: shepards-data
observations: Shepard's Citations: 5,711,699 citation treatment edges in shepards_edges table
9 treatment types: cites (5M), follows (366K), distinguishes (143K), explains (89K), questions (15K), criticizes (4K), overrules (1K), limits (809)
treatment_norm field with polarity: positive (follows), negative (distinguishes/questions/criticizes/overrules/limits), neutral (cites/explains)
agree boolean indicates whether citing case agrees with cited case's holding
Essential for FDQ-01 KA-SC polarity, FDQ-05 DISTINGUISH, FDQ-08 TRANSITIVE, FDQ-07 UNKNOWN-AUTH

name: citation-views
observations: scotus_citations_ranked_flat: 293,816 rows with rank, fowler_score, occurrences, resolved status per citation
cap_citations_ranked_flat: 50,364 rows for federal authority citations with pagerank_percentile
Join patterns: anchor_usCite/cited_usCite → scdb_cases.usCite → shepards_edges via lexisCite
Primary query targets for FDQ-01 KA-SC step and Research Pack selection

name: fdq-01-ka-sc
observations: Position 1 (always first), Known Authority family, tests citation landscape comprehension
4 sub-questions: controlling_authority (highest Fowler), in_favor (follows treatment), against (negative treatments), most_frequent (highest occurrences)
Scoring: 25% exact match + 25% F1 + 25% F1 + 25% exact match
Data: scotus_citations_ranked_flat + shepards_edges + scdb_cases
Eligibility: ~15K+ anchors with non-null Fowler scores and Shepard's treatment edges

name: fdq-02-c-nonexist1
observations: Position 2, Canary family, tests hallucination resistance with fabricated cases
Synthetic citation generation: deterministic algorithm using EU seed (volume 400-550, year 1970-2005)
Ground truth: REFUSE; Scoring: Pattern-based classifier REFUSAL vs ASSERTION patterns

name: fdq-03-canary
observations: Position 3, Canary family, tests information discipline with PASSING citations (real cases, text NOT provided)
Citation depth labels: DETAILED (full text in RP, TF-IDF >0.15) vs PASSING (only mentioned)
Ground truth: REFUSE; Scoring: classifier with HEDGED_HALLUCINATION patterns

name: fdq-04-fact-extract
observations: Position 4, Fact Extraction family, tests SCDB code interpretation with closed enums
2 sub-questions: reversal (TRUE/FALSE from caseDisposition 3,4,5,8), prevailing_party (PETITIONER/RESPONDENT/UNCLEAR)
Scoring: 50% exact match each; Eligibility: ~28K cases (99%+ SCDB) with non-null codes

name: fdq-05-distinguish
observations: Position 5, Citation Treatment family, tests single-edge treatment recognition
2 sub-questions: treatment (8-value enum), agree (TRUE/FALSE) from Shepard's
Selection: highest-authority cited case by Fowler score with Shepard's edge
Scoring: 50% exact match for treatment, 50% for agree
Coverage: ~50K+ citation pairs with Shepard's treatment edges

name: fdq-06-validate-auth
observations: Position 6, Citation Validation family, tests cross-source consistency
4-value enum: CONSISTENT, INCONSISTENT, CURATED_ONLY, CITATOR_ONLY
Compares treatment polarity between curated list and citator database

name: fdq-07-unknown-auth
observations: Position 7, Citation Discovery family, tests reverse citation lookup
Task: list up to 10 SCOTUS cases that cite given precedent
MRR scoring: 1/rank of first correct (1.0 if first, 0.5 if second, 0.0 if none in top 10)
Eligibility: precedents cited by ≥3 SCOTUS cases for meaningful ranking

name: fdq-08-transitive
observations: Position 8, Citation Reasoning family, tests multi-edge inference over A←B←C triangles
Task: predict C→A treatment given B→A and C→B treatments
Ground truth: actual C→A Shepard's edge (NOT theoretical expectation)
Scoring: 50% exact match treatment, 50% agree
Eligibility: complete triangles with all 3 edges and temporal order A < B < C

name: fdq-09-irac-no-rp
observations: Position 9, Legal Writing family, closed-book IRAC using only anchor opinion
4 IRAC components: Issue, Rule, Application, Conclusion - strict JSON format
Scoring: MEE rubric 0-6 per component, judge grades AFTER j10
Citation discipline: must appear in anchor_inventory_full

name: fdq-10-irac-with-rp
observations: Position 10, Legal Writing family, open-book IRAC with Research Pack (p2 admitted)
RP provides Top-K=10 SCOTUS (Fowler) + Top-K=5 CAP (PageRank) authorities
Scoring: MEE rubric, judge grades BOTH d9+j10 in single call
Citation discipline: may be in anchor_inventory OR rp_subset

name: canary-mechanism
observations: Distinguishes DETAILED (full text, TF-IDF >0.15) from PASSING citations (only mentioned)
Canary questions ask about PASSING citations - correct answer is refusal
Tests information discipline: only use admitted evidence, not pretraining

name: candidate-state
observations: candidate_state.json carries model-derived artifacts between steps, keyed by step_id
Sanitization strips keys containing: ground_truth, score, judge, gt_, rubric before carry-forward
Accumulates (never removes) enabling chained reasoning
Per-EU scope - no cross-EU state leakage

name: payload-admission
observations: plan.json inject_payloads is SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for evidence visibility
Payloads cumulative - once admitted, remain visible
Typical: p1 (anchor) at d1, p2 (research pack) at j10
Reference to unadmitted payload = runtime error
Placeholder dot notation: {p1.anchor.text}, {p2.authorities[0]} for deep path resolution

name: research-pack-selection
observations: SCOTUS: Top-K=10 by Fowler pauth_score DESC, tie-break: occurrences DESC → lexicographic
CAP: Top-K=5 by PageRank percentile DESC
Combined typical RP: 15 authorities (10 SCOTUS + 5 federal)
Deterministic selection ensures reproducible Research Packs

name: staging-isolation
observations: Structural no-leak via staging directories: staging/{run_id}/{call_id}/
Model can ONLY read files copied into per-call staging
Ground truth, judge prompts, future steps NEVER staged
Core security guarantee: filesystem path to unauthorized content doesn't exist

name: legal-10-benchmark
observations: Runner-orchestrated benchmark evaluating LLM ability to perform stateful multi-step legal reasoning over sealed staged evidence sets
Tests closed-book reasoning from anchor SCOTUS opinion then open-book synthesis after admitting Research Pack
Key insight: reveals gap between isolated task performance (~90% accuracy) and end-to-end chain reliability (~35% completion due to error propagation)
Live demo at legalchain.run with 24 tested models
10-step chain: d1-d8 deterministic + d9/j10 judge-scored IRACs + post-chain actions
Currently at ~90% completion with 3-step MVP as immediate priority

name: audit-log-proves-visibility
observations: audit_log.jsonl contains hash proofs for verification of what model could see
Pre-call: hash staged files and assembled messages; Post-call: record with step_id, timestamp
Enables 'what did candidate see?' verification for audit and reproducibility
Combined with run_manifest.json file_hashes for complete provenance

name: candidate-state-enables-chaining
observations: candidate_state.json is ONLY mechanism for passing artifacts between steps
OutputHarvester extracts and sanitizes step output before adding to state
InputAssembler includes CARRY_FORWARD window with prior state
State keyed by step_id allows later steps to reference earlier outputs

name: cap-provides-federal-texts
observations: cap_cases_meta provides metadata for 855K federal cases as secondary authorities
cap_text_stats includes pagerank_percentile for CAP RP selection (Top-K=5)
cap_head_matter.jsonl provides head matter text for p2.json authorities
Coverage: F.1d, F.2d, F.3d, F.Supp series - excludes F.4th

name: citation-inventory-validates
observations: anchor_inventory_full lists all in-scope citations from anchor text
rp_subset lists citations shipped in Research Pack (p2.json)
d9 validation: against anchor_inventory only
j10 validation: against anchor_inventory OR rp_subset

name: data-joins
observations: scdb_cases.lexisCite → fowler_scores.lexis_cite (LEFT JOIN for scdb_with_fowler)
scdb_cases.majOpinWriter+term → martin_quinn_scores (INNER JOIN for scdb_with_ideology)
scotus_citations_ranked_flat → scdb_cases via anchor_usCite/cited_usCite
shepards_edges.citing_lexis/cited_lexis → scdb_cases.lexisCite

name: eu-builder-produces-packets
observations: EU Builder generates sealed evaluation unit packets from database at build time
Produces p1.json (anchor), p2.json (RP authorities), ground_truth.json (scoring data)
SHA-256 hashes computed at build for reproducibility - stored in run_manifest.json
No runtime retrieval - all evidence pre-packaged for sealed evaluation

name: fdq-data-dependencies
observations: d1 KA-SC: scotus_citations_ranked_flat + shepards_edges
d2/d3 Canary: synthetic citations (d2) or citation_labels depth (d3)
d4 FACT-EXTRACT: scdb_cases.caseDisposition + partyWinning
d5-d8: shepards_edges for treatment, triangles, reverse lookup
d9/j10 IRAC: anchor text + Research Pack with MEE judge scoring

name: fowler-ranks-authorities
observations: fowler_scores.pauth_score determines SCOTUS RP selection (Top-K=10 by authority)
Higher score = more influential case via citation network PageRank analysis
FDQ-01 controlling_authority uses highest Fowler score as ground truth
Join via lexis_cite to scdb_cases.lexisCite for SCOTUS resolution

name: ground-truth-enables-scoring
observations: ground_truth.json contains step_ground_truth keyed by step_id
citation_labels provide depth_label (DETAILED/PASSING) and shepard_treatment
integrity_gate provides canonical_citations and synthetic_traps
Ground truth is RUNNER-ONLY - never staged, never visible to model

name: judge-grading-flow
observations: d9 produces IRAC_A (closed-book), j10 produces IRAC_B (open-book)
Single judge call after j10 grades BOTH using MEE rubric
Judge isolated from: ground_truth, candidate transcript, step prompts
Enables direct closed-book vs open-book comparison

name: judge-model-isolated
observations: Judge runs as SEPARATE Inspect Task with complete isolation
Judge sees ONLY: judge prompt rubric + model output via {response} placeholder
Judge does NOT see: candidate transcript, ground_truth, other steps, runner internals
Component scores aggregated by RUNNER, not judge - runner maintains scoring authority

name: langfuse-receives-traces
observations: Langfuse is observation platform receiving traces from external runners via hooks
PDRunner emits trace events (step_start, model_call, step_complete) via LangfuseHooks
Architecture: Langfuse (UI/Auth/DB) ← traces ← PDRunner ← Inspect
Dataset model fit for EU packets needs evaluation

name: pdrunner-wraps-inspect
observations: Inspect-AI: intra-step execution (model calls, Plan/Solver, Scorer registry)
PDRunner: inter-task orchestration (PayloadGate, InputAssembler, OutputHarvester, AuditBoundary)
Each AGChain step = one inspect.eval() call
~300 LOC thin wrapper enforcing Legal-10 invariants

name: plan-json-controls-admission
observations: plan.json inject_payloads array is SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for payload visibility
PayloadGate reads inject_payloads to determine which files to stage
Cumulative admission: once admitted, payload remains staged for all subsequent steps
Runtime error if step prompt references unadmitted payload

name: rp-ranking-sources
observations: fowler_scores.pauth_score ranks SCOTUS RP authorities (Top-K=10)
cap_text_stats.pagerank_percentile ranks CAP RP authorities (Top-K=5)
Deterministic tie-breaking: occurrences DESC → lexicographic ASC

name: scdb-provides-anchor-metadata
observations: scdb_cases provides 55 columns of case characteristics for anchor selection
FACT-EXTRACT uses caseDisposition, partyWinning as ground truth
scdb_with_fowler joins with authority scores for RP selection criteria
SCDB caseId links to citation inventories, text files, and other datasets

name: scoring-assignments
observations: d1: exact-match (25%) + F1 (50%) + exact-match (25%)
d2/d3: classifier (PASS/FAIL)
d4/d5/d6/d8: exact-match
d7: MRR
d9/j10: MEE judge rubric + citation integrity post-check

name: security-chain
observations: plan.json inject_payloads → PayloadGate → staging directory (only admitted files)
staging isolation → model can only read staged files → ground_truth inaccessible
candidate_state sanitization → strips GT/scores before carry-forward
audit_log.jsonl hash proofs → verifiable record of what model could see

name: shepards-provides-treatments
observations: shepards_edges provides ground truth for citation treatment classification
DISTINGUISH (d5) uses treatment_norm to verify model's treatment identification
KA polarity uses agree field to classify citations as in_favor vs against
Treatment distribution: cites 5M, follows 366K, distinguishes 143K, explains 89K

name: staging-enforces-isolation
observations: staging/{run_id}/{call_id}/ physically isolates model from ground_truth, judge_prompts, future steps
Model adapter can ONLY read from staging - no filesystem path exists to other files
Ground truth is NEVER copied to staging - core security guarantee
Staging cleanup after each step prevents cross-step file accumulation

name: synthetic-traps-detect-fabrication
observations: 50K global synthetic trap pool with deterministic fake citations
2 traps injected per EU from global pool
Citation integrity check compares model citations against trap set
Match indicates fabrication/hallucination
Trap structural tells: impossible U.S. Reports volumes, future dates, implausible party names

name: scoring-citation-integrity
observations: Post-chain check validating IRAC citations against admitted inventories
d9: anchor_inventory_full only; j10: anchor_inventory OR rp_subset
Failures as penalties (not voiding) - evaluation proceeds to completion

name: scoring-classifier
observations: Pattern-based classifier: PASS (1.0) vs FAIL (0.0)
Used by: FDQ-02 C-NONEXIST1 and FDQ-03 CANARY
Patterns: REFUSAL (cannot find), ASSERTION (court held), HEDGED (I believe)

name: scoring-exact-match
observations: Binary: 1.0 if normalized match, 0.0 otherwise
Used by: FDQ-04 (reversal+party), FDQ-05 (treatment+agree), FDQ-06, FDQ-08
Normalization: uppercase, strip whitespace, pin cite stripping

name: scoring-f1
observations: F1 = 2*(precision*recall)/(precision+recall) for list responses
Used by: FDQ-01 KA-SC for in_favor and against lists (25% weight each)
Citation normalization: canonical_key strips punctuation, collapses whitespace

name: scoring-mee-judge
observations: MEE rubric: 0-6 per IRAC component (Issue/Rule/Application/Conclusion)
Used by: FDQ-09 and FDQ-10, judge grades BOTH in single call after j10
Judge isolated: sees ONLY IRAC outputs, NOT ground truth or transcript

name: scoring-mrr
observations: Mean Reciprocal Rank: 1/rank of first correct answer
Used by: FDQ-07 UNKNOWN-AUTH for reverse citation lookup
Score: 1.0=first correct, 0.5=second, 0.0=none in top 10

name: benchmark-package
observations: benchmark/: benchmark.json, plan.json, model_steps/*.json, judge_steps/*.json
eus/{eu_id}/: p1.json, p2.json, ground_truth.json (runner-only)
runs/{run_id}/: run.jsonl, audit_log.jsonl, run_manifest.json, summary.json
Step prefixes: d* deterministic, j* judge-required, p* payloads

name: eu-packet
observations: p1.json: anchor (caseId, usCite, caseName, full text) + metadata.citations
p2.json: authorities array (citation_id, usCite, text) + metadata.authority_scores
ground_truth.json (runner-only): step_ground_truth, citation_labels, integrity_gate with synthetic_traps
Synthetic trap structural tells: impossible U.S. Reports volumes, cases with future dates, implausible party names

name: inter-step-requirements
observations: State transition: candidate_state keyed by step_id, accumulates, JSON-serializable
Payload admission: inject_payloads controls timing, cumulative once admitted
Pre-step: staging + hash + message assembly; Post-step: extract + validate + score + state update + audit
Concurrency: steps within EU sequential; multiple EUs may run in parallel

name: message-assembly
observations: Fenced windows: <<<BEGIN_{NAME}>>>...<<<END_{NAME}>>>
Order: ENV → ANCHOR_PACK → EVIDENCE_PACK → CARRY_FORWARD → TASK → OUTPUT_GUARD
Session strategies: Replay_Full vs Replay_Minimal - both yield identical audit proofs
Judge prompt truncation: >50k chars truncated

name: open-decisions
observations: Anchor text budget: filter-only vs runtime truncation
KA citation scope: SCOTUS-only vs +federal; pin cite normalization
Canary classification for ambiguous/hedged responses
Composite score aggregation weights (currently arithmetic mean)

name: pdrunner-execution
observations: Phase 1-3: Create staging, stage files, hash for audit, assemble messages with windows
Phase 4-6: Create Task/Sample, call inspect.eval(), validate against output_contract, execute scorer
Phase 7-9: Sanitize output, update candidate_state, emit run/audit records, delete staging
Sample.metadata must NEVER contain ground_truth - Inspect passes to TaskState.metadata (model-visible)

name: plan-json
observations: Single source of truth: step ordering, scoring mode, scorer_ref, judge_prompt_file, output_contract, inject_payloads
Required fields: step_id, step_file, scoring ('deterministic' or 'judge'), output_contract
Steps execute in array order regardless of step_id labels

name: post-chain-actions
observations: Judge Evaluation: separate call AFTER j10, grades BOTH d9 and j10 IRACs in single evaluation
Judge isolation: sees ONLY IRAC outputs, NOT ground truth, transcript, or runner internals
Citation Integrity: extracts citations from IRAC JSON, validates against inventories
Failures as penalties, do NOT void scores - evaluation proceeds to completion

name: reporter-scope
observations: SCOTUS in scope: U.S. Reports
Federal in scope: F., F.2d, F.3d, F.Supp., F.Supp.2d, F.Supp.3d
F.4th EXCLUDED everywhere; State reporters OUT OF SCOPE
CAP distribution: cap_f2d (275K), cap_f1supp (214K), cap_f3d (141K), cap_f2supp (115K), cap_f1d (72K), cap_f3supp (37K)

name: resolved-decisions
observations: Synthetic trap pool: 50K global fake citations, 2 per EU
Top-K: SCOTUS K=10 by Fowler, CAP K=5 by PageRank
Judge records inside run.jsonl; Citation integrity penalty-based (no voiding)
Anchor opinions NEVER trimmed at runtime

name: run-artifacts
observations: run.jsonl: per-step records with raw_output, parsed, validation, score, latency_ms, tokens_used
audit_log.jsonl: hash proofs binding staged bytes + message bytes
run_manifest.json: provenance with benchmark_id, eu_id, runner_version, file_hashes
summary.json: step_scores, aggregate_score, chain_completion, integrity_check
